data.purpose: Counts, badges, filenames, errors. Mono means data; tabular-nums.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Every transition and entrance; no bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: progress
motion.purpose: Determinate progress fill (transform: scaleX, never width).

motion.name: drag-feedback
motion.purpose: Selection rings, insertion lines, merge outlines, drag dimming — the editable surface has no entrance choreography.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single gold action on a screen; hover deepens, active nudges down.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; color: oklch(0.235 0.05 75.1); background: oklch(0.817 0.161 75.1); border: none; border-radius: 7px; padding: 8px 18px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.775 0.155 75.1); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0.5px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.56 0.125 75.1); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Quiet Button
components.description: Secondary action: hairline border, surface fill on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-quiet { font-family: -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.19 0 0); background: transparent; border: 1px solid oklch(0.89 0 0); border-radius: 7px; padding: 6px 14px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-quiet:hover { background: oklch(0.955 0 0); } .ds-btn-quiet:active { background: oklch(0.89 0 0); } .ds-btn-quiet:disabled { opacity: 0.45; cursor: default; } .ds-btn-quiet:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.56 0.125 75.1); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Progress Bar
components.description: 3px determinate bar; fill scales via transform, paired with a mono count.
components.css: .ds-progress { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 10px; } .ds-progress-track { width: 240px; height: 3px; border-radius: 2px; background: oklch(0.955 0 0); overflow: hidden; } .ds-progress-fill { height: 100%; width: 100%; border-radius: 2px; background: oklch(0.56 0.125 75.1); transform: scaleX(0.55); transform-origin: left; } .ds-progress-count { font-family: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: oklch(0.47 0 0); }

components.name: Group Row
components.description: One cluster on the light table: mono index + badge line over a wrapping thumbnail row.
components.html: 23 photos · b&w
components.css: .ds-group { display: block; } .ds-group-head { display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 12px; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-g-index { font-family: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; color: oklch(0.19 0 0); } .ds-g-meta { font-family: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; color: oklch(0.47 0 0); } .ds-g-row { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; } .ds-thumb-ph { height: 176px; border-radius: 5px; background: linear-gradient(160deg, oklch(0.9 0 0), oklch(0.82 0 0)); }

components.name: Failure Disclosure
components.description: Quiet native details element listing unreadable files; never a modal.
components.html:
1 file couldn’t be read
  • broken.jpg — not an image
components.css: .ds-failures { font-family: -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; color: oklch(0.47 0 0); } .ds-failures summary { cursor: pointer; width: fit-content; } .ds-failures summary:hover { color: oklch(0.19 0 0); } .ds-failures ul { margin: 8px 0 0; padding-left: 24px; } .ds-mono { font-family: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; }

components.name: Error Message
components.description: Mono error text in darkroom red, always beside a recovery action.
components.html: reading /nonexistent/delivery: No such file or directory
components.css: .ds-error { font-family: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; color: oklch(0.5 0.19 27); max-width: 60ch; overflow-wrap: anywhere; margin: 0; }

narrative.overview: Sequin is a photographer's light table: a quiet, glowing surface where the shoot is laid out, grouped, and slid into order. The photographs are the interface — chrome recedes to near-nothing, thumbnails carry all the color and meaning, and the app's own voice appears only where the photos can't speak: counts, badges, and one honey-gold glint on the single action that matters. The app follows macOS appearance: light mode is the white light table, dark mode is the darkroom — surfaces drop to pure near-black so thumbnails glow.
keyCharacteristics: Photos-first: every screen judged by how much of it is photograph
Dual-theme, pure chroma-0 neutral surfaces; all warmth lives in the honey-gold accent
One quiet system sans for UI; monospace strictly for data
Responsive motion (150–280ms, ease-out expo), flat at rest
WCAG 2.1 AA: contrast per theme, reduced-motion alternatives, keyboard-reachable controls

rules.name: The Ten-Percent Rule
rules.body: Honey gold touches at most 10% of any screen. If gold appears on more than the primary action, the progress bar, focus rings, and (future) selection, it has leaked.

rules.name: The Pure Surface Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are chroma-0 in both themes. Tinted-cream or tinted-charcoal backgrounds are prohibited; the photographs supply all environmental color.

rules.name: The Mono-Means-Data Rule
rules.body: Monospace is reserved for values the app reads or writes. UI labels never wear it; data never wears the sans.

rules.name: The Drag-Lift Rule
rules.body: Nothing casts a shadow at rest — ever. During a drag the lift is carried by the system drag ghost; the source cell dims and the destination is drawn by a 2px gold line. The app draws no shadows of its own.

dos: Do let thumbnails dominate: maximize photograph-per-pixel on every screen.
Do use Apple Photos' familiar grid conventions — earned familiarity over invention.
Do keep transitions 150–280ms with cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) and give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion alternative.
Do give every interactive element default, hover, focus-visible, active, and disabled states, keyboard-reachable.
Do pair every error with a recovery action.
donts: Don't build the "pro-app panel forest" — no docked panels, no control walls.
Don't build the "generic web dashboard" — no SaaS cards, badges, or decorative shadows; nothing that reads "website in a window".
Don't slide into "sterile minimalism" — no mystery-meat icons, no unlabeled functions, no form over usability.
Don't tint the surfaces; The Pure Surface Rule stands. Warmth is honey gold's job.
Don't put bright honey gold on white for lines or text — use Deep Gold there.
Don't decorate with motion. If an animation doesn't convey state, feedback, or the single completion reward, it doesn't exist.
